title: uxgetc
text: uxgetc Get additional field data from the I/O buffer. Explain this code short uxgetc (unsigned char*buffer, shortmax); Copy Parameters buffer Data from the I/O buffer (Output) max Size of the buffer pointed to by buffer . This value should not be larger than the actual size of the buffer. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uxgetc Value Meaning >0 The number of bytes copied into the buffer 0 An error occurred <0 The negated number of bytes copied into the buffer. The negative status indicates that more data is available in the I/O buffer, and that you should call uxgetc to get the additional data. Use Library: yrtl Description uxgetc moves an additional chunk of field data from the I/O buffer to the specified program buffer. This function should be called only after a previous call to uxget or uxgetc returned a negative value, indicating there is more data available. Parameters and return values are the same as for uxget . So if uxgetc returns a negative status, it must be called repeatedly until a positive status is returned. Profile and subfield separator characters, if present in a field, are also returned in the buffer, so you should be prepared to handle these. For information about the representation of these characters, see the "Subfield Separator and Profile Characters" topic. Example: uxget and uxgetc The following example shows how to use uxget and uxgetc to obtain field data from the I/O buffer. Because the size of the field is assumed unknown, we read fixed chunks and expand the buffer as necessary. We use 100 as the size of the fixed chunks to read, although this is an arbitrary choice. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #define CHUNKSIZE 100 EXPORT int getbuffer () { char* buf; int len; int totlen; len = uxget(buf, CHUNKSIZE); /* Get the first chunk of data */ totlen = abs(len); /* Set total length */ while ( len < 0 ) /* More to come */ { buf = realloc(buf, totlen+CHUNKSIZE); /* Make room for next chunk*/ len = uxgetc(buf+totlen, CHUNKSIZE); /* Read next chunk */ totlen += abs(len); /* Update total length */ } return totlen; } Copy Related Links Subfield Separator and Profile Characters Uniface uses part of the reserved area of each meta character set (that is, the first 32 characters plus the last character) to store characters with special meaning within Uniface, such as subfield separators and profile characters. uxget Copy field data from the I/O buffer to the specified program buffer.
